problems.name: Systemic lupus erythematosus, active with inflammatory myositis
problems.diagnosedBy: Initial 1/12/2026 (urgent care); diagnosis evolved through 2026; myositis newly diagnosed at 2/8/2027 ED
problems.notes: Trajectory: presented 1/12/2026 as undifferentiated polyarthritis with 1:80 ANA. Rheum (4/10/2026) called it UCTD / probable early SLE. By 11/4/2026 met more SLE criteria (anti-dsDNA newly positive, low complements, cytopenias, photosensitive rash, weight loss). On MMF + HCQ + prednisone, improved by 12/22/2026 BUT developed new dysphagia (foreshadowing myositis). 2/8/2027 ED admission revealed inflammatory myositis (CK 1840, aldolase 22) with oropharyngeal dysphagia. Discharged AMA 2/11. Returned 3/15/2027 in catastrophic state.

problems.name: Severe oropharyngeal dysphagia (presumed myositis-related)
problems.diagnosedBy: First documented 12/22/2026 (Dr. Williams, PCP); MBSS confirmed 2/9/2027
problems.notes: Onset late 2026 as bolus dysphagia with dry foods. Progressed through early 2027 to involve liquids. MBSS 2/9/2027 showed mild oropharyngeal dysphagia with laryngeal penetration of thin liquids — recommended nectar-thick liquids + chin-tuck. By 3/15/2027 admission, patient is unable to manage solids; mostly on Ensure with limited intake. Drives the malnutrition. Workup repeats MBSS this admission.

problems.name: Inflammatory myositis (presumed SLE-related; possible DM overlap)
problems.diagnosedBy: CK elevation first noted 12/2026 lab; confirmed at 2/8/2027 ED
problems.notes: CK trajectory: 412 (12/15/2026) -> 638 (1/12/2027) -> 1840 (2/8/2027) -> 1620 (2/11) -> 1490 (2/15) -> 2360 (3/15/2027). Aldolase elevated throughout. Proximal weakness escalating from 4+/5 (2/8) to 2-3/5 (3/15). Dysphagia and dysphonia consistent with pharyngeal/bulbar involvement. EMG and muscle biopsy NOT YET performed (planned this admission). Skin biopsy 2/10 read consistent with interface dermatitis with kwashiorkor features. Myositis-specific antibody panel sent 3/15.

problems.name: Severe protein-calorie malnutrition (kwashiorkor pattern)
problems.notes: Severe malnutrition with multi-modal evidence: 30.3% weight loss from baseline (50.8 -> 35.4 kg); albumin 1.6 (today, 3/15); prealbumin 6 (severely low); anasarca; scaling dermatosis with 'flaky paint' pattern; 'flag sign' hair; Beau's lines; low zinc, copper, vitamin D. Refeeding-syndrome risk (low K, Mg, Phos). Initiating slow refeeding protocol per nutrition consult.

problems.name: Alopecia, diffuse non-scarring
problems.notes: Diffuse non-scarring scalp hair thinning. Progressed; 'flag sign' visible 3/15/2027 — consistent with nutritional contribution.

problems.name: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (autoimmune)
problems.notes: Patient on levothyroxine 25 mcg daily with intermittent adherence. TSH 6.84, free T4 0.6 today (3/15/2027). Overlying non-thyroidal illness pattern. May need dose adjustment once stabilized.

problems.name: Loss of health insurance coverage
problems.notes: Medicaid lost in 2020. Re-application denied 5/29/2026 + 11/20/2026. Emergency Medicaid coverage active for 3/15/2027 admission. Persistent driver of care fragmentation.

problems.name: Medication non-adherence due to access barriers
problems.notes: Recurrent: HCQ gaps 8/12-9/15/2026 and 10/15-11/4/2026. MMF ran out 3/9/2027. TMP/SMX taken intermittently. Repeated medication interruption is a major driver of disease progression.

problems.name: Unintentional weight loss / severe cachexia
problems.notes: Trajectory: 50.8 kg (1/12/26) -> 35.4 kg (3/15/27). Total loss 15.4 kg / 30.3%. BMI 15.7. Underweight per WHO criteria. Driver of admission. See PRB010.

problems.name: Depression, moderate
problems.notes: PHQ-9 11 -> 14 -> 12 -> 7 (improved with treatment response) -> unable to obtain on 3/15/2027 admission due to acuity. Seeing Esperanza Behavioral Health therapist; declined antidepressant. Hopkins psych-CL planned this admission.

problems.name: Pancytopenia (tri-lineage)
problems.diagnosedBy: Trajectory: leukopenia from 9/15/2026, anemia + thrombocytopenia worsening through 2026-2027
problems.notes: Multifactorial: SLE-related cytopenias, mycophenolate marrow suppression, nutritional deficiency (low B12 borderline, low folate borderline, iron-deficiency component). Hgb 7.8, WBC 2.30, Plt 122 on 3/15/2027 admission. Reticulocyte count low (0.6%).

problems.name: Lupus nephritis (early — class undetermined)
problems.notes: Progressive proteinuria with normal creatinine (low muscle mass distorts). Renal biopsy deferred pending stabilization; consideration this admission.

reviewedBy: Dr. Sarah Beckman / Dr. Maria Castro / Dr. David Park — 3/15/2027 admission H&P
